name: MiniMax-M2
archDisplay: 62-layer decoder-only Transformer, 256 experts (top-8, sigmoid gating) + Multi-Token Prediction module, full GQA attention
note: Compact 229.9B-total/9.8B-active MoE tuned for coding and agentic workflows with interleaved <think> reasoning; original M2 release is MIT-licensed (later M2.x point releases, e.g. M2.1/M2.7, moved to a restrictive 'Modified-MIT').
tpsNote: Unsloth's UD-IQ4_XS dynamic GGUF (~108GB) is reported at ~15-25 tok/s on a single 16GB GPU + 96GB system RAM or Mac unified memory - no RTX-4090-specific figure found.

results.notes: GPQA-Diamond; identical figure independently confirmed on the HF model card's 'Intelligence Benchmarks' table.
BrowseComp (English); confirmed identically on the HF model card's 'Coding & Agentic Benchmarks' table. A separate BrowseComp-zh score of 48.5 also exists but has no distinct taxonomy slug.
Confirmed identically on the HF model card.

τ²-Bench-Telecom, confirmed identically on the HF model card. Distinct from the generic τ²-Bench (77.2) also reported, which mixes airline/retail/telecom domains and doesn't cleanly match either the tau-bench or tau2-bench-telecom taxonomy definitions, so that figure was left out.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real; official minimax.io/news/minimax-m2 post is literally dated 2025.10.27, matching releaseDate exactly (MarkTechPost/Simon Willison coverage followed a day or two later). 229.9B total/9.8B active MIT-licensed MoE confirmed via GitHub repo and MarkTechPost. All 7 benchmark rows (SWE-bench 69.4, LiveCodeBench 83, MMLU-Pro 82, AIME25 78, Terminal-Bench 46.3, GAIA 75.7, HLE 31.8) confirmed literally on the GitHub README. Independently confirmed the note's claim that later M2.x point releases (M2.1, M2.7) carry a distinct 'Modified-MIT' license requiring UI attribution, unlike the base M2's plain MIT.

2026-07-16: Researched the four missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, mmmu) for MiniMax-M2; all four resolve to omitted_no_evidence, no new results added. Checked the GitHub README and HF model card (both list 'Intelligence Benchmarks': MMLU-Pro, AIME25, GPQA-Diamond, LiveCodeBench, SciCode, IFBench, HLE, AA-LCR, τ²-Bench-Telecom, Terminal-Bench-Hard — no legacy MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval row in either), artificialanalysis.ai/models/minimax-m2 (same benchmark set, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval), the official minimax.io/news/minimax-m2 launch post (names no individual benchmarks at all), and the MiniMax-M2 Series arXiv technical report (arxiv.org/abs/2605.26494), whose only MATH (24.1) / HumanEval (32.5) figures belong to a small 2B-active ablation/proxy model used during architecture development, not the released M2 flagship, and whose main M2.5/M2.7 comparison table (Table 4) likewise omits standalone MMLU/MATH/HumanEval for M2. Vals.ai only has pages for later point releases (M2.1/M2.5/M2.7), none of which report these legacy metrics either. This is consistent with MiniMax's stated preference for MMLU-Pro/AIME/LiveCodeBench-era benchmarks over saturated legacy ones. MMMU is additionally explained by the model being confirmed text-only (capabilities.vision: false, modalities: ['text']) with no vision encoder — MMMU is a multimodal benchmark and was never applicable to this model.

2026-07-18: Re-confirmed MMMU gap remains architecturally inapplicable (not re-searched further per task rule, since corpus fields are dispositive and a prior 2026-07-16 pass already reached the same conclusion after multi-source checking). MiniMax-M2's own corpus record states modalities: ["text"] and capabilities.vision: false, with archDisplay confirming a pure text decoder-only MoE (62-layer, 256 experts + MTP module) with no vision encoder or image-input pathway. MMMU (Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding) requires image inputs paired with text questions, so it cannot be run against a model with no vision modality at all -- this is a hard architectural mismatch, not a reporting gap. No credible source would report an MMMU score for a text-only model since the benchmark is inapplicable to its input surface. Conclusion: omitted_no_evidence, reason "architecturally inapplicable (text-only model, no vision modality/encoder)".
